title: A Two-Edged Sword
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp}}
{{ShortStory

|title="A Two-Edged Sword"

"'''A Two-Edged Sword'''," erroneously printed as "'''Two-Edged Sword''',"<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=01230#13479250|thread=Welcome some BCaT VIPs|user=[[Karen Traviss]]|date=2005-12-03|time=2:06 PM|quote=A Two-Edged Sword. With an A. I haven't even seen the magazine so I don't know what happened there. Things slip out sometimes. Like the intro to In His Image got moved and ended up as an anchor on the illustration page and people didn't realise it was part of the story. (The quote from Palpatine about it being inevitable and natural that Vader will try to overthrow him one day.) If it ever gets reprinted, I hope we can sort that.}}</ref> is a short story in [[Star Wars Insider 85|''Star Wars Insider'' 85]] written by [[Karen Traviss]]. It is the sequel to "[[In His Image]]." Both were included in the paperback version of ''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''.

It follows [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] training clones for the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] corps and explores Vader's thoughts of betrayal and paranoia. It is set in about [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]].

==Plot summary==
Vader is on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] training clones of [[Sa Cuis]] in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], with the Emperor and Imperial [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Erv Lekauf]]. In addition various [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] were cloned and trained, particularly the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Sheyvan]]. Returning to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Sheyvan and the Cuis clones revolt on Palpatine's [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]].

[[File:Stormtrooper_Saber_art.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] engages the [[Cuis clone|stormtroopers]].]]

Vader makes his way to the revolt and overhears Sheyvan yelling that Palpatine will betray them all. Vader disregards this and plans with Lekauf, [[Pepin]], and [[Nele]] to storm the bulkhead with [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]] before Vader enters to finish them off. In the chaos Lekauf is burned by a backlash of fire, and is subsequently given [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]]. After entering the bulkhead, Vader fights with Sheyvan and kills him. Lekauf survives and returns to his family on Coruscant; his injuries remind Vader of his former self.

Vader is so impressed by Lekauf's actions that he orders more Lekauf clones be created.
